approval.note: Repository implementation is authorized by the user. Runtime recreation is allowed only after merge, immutable-image verification, data-path preflight and explicit rollback evidence; no data deletion or image pruning.

title: Pin local utility container images and canonicalize Compose sources
goal: Make the existing Heim-PC utility container stack reproducible without an implicit upgrade by pinning the currently proven images, adding a canonical Beszel-agent Compose source, validating against floating tags, and documenting controlled update, rollout and rollback paths.

acceptance.assertion: Backrest, Beszel hub and agent, Stirling PDF, Paperless, Postgres and Redis are pinned to immutable registry digests matching the image contents proven live before the change; readable version tags are retained where registry resolution proves the same digest.
The Beszel agent Compose definition has a canonical secret-free source in infra and a deterministic install/render path for the runtime location under ~/.local/share/heim-utilities.
Infra validation rejects latest or otherwise unpinned image references in the canonical Heim-PC utility Compose sources.
The runbook records preflight, backup/data boundaries, staged recreation order, health checks and exact rollback to the previous image references without deleting data.
After merge, the running containers are recreated from the canonical infra source rather than an obsolete worktree, while the separate agent stack records its canonical source digest.
Beszel hub and agent, Backrest, Stirling PDF and Paperless pass bounded post-rollout health checks; Paperless database and Redis remain healthy; no browser-facing port becomes non-loopback.

metadata.live_finding: The live utility stack used floating image tags; three containers were still attributed to an obsolete infra worktree and the Beszel agent Compose file had no canonical repository source.
validation: Infra PR #113 GitHub validate passed on head 51d5af914e263da476cb9f5798b44ec21bc8bba4.
Repeated local make validate and ShellCheck passed before merge.
All seven readable version tags resolved to the exact manifests already running before rollout.
Staged live recreation passed for Stirling PDF, Backrest, Beszel hub and agent, PostgreSQL, Redis and Paperless.

Post-rollout verifier passed immutable digests, canonical Compose provenance, loopback bindings, no published database/broker ports, PostgreSQL/Redis readiness, HTTP services, agent receipt and Beszel monitoring.
Paperless document/user counts remained 2|3 before and after recreation.
Infra PR #114 validate passed and published the redacted runtime evidence.
metadata.outcome: The Heim-PC utility stack now runs the previously proven image contents through immutable version+digest references. Main services use the canonical Infra Compose source; the Beszel agent uses a canonical installed source and unique project identity. Runtime and rollback evidence are preserved without data deletion or image pruning.

verified_acceptance.current-image-pins: All seven services run immutable version+digest references whose manifest digests equal the pre-rollout image contents.
verified_acceptance.canonical-agent-source: Infra owns compose/heim-utilities/beszel-agent.compose.yml; the atomic installer and source receipt bind the runtime copy to source SHA-256 e0ae0f58a6a751dc93df05948bade342d57bfadfd9f75e4808bc0d9fca7c3df8.
verified_acceptance.floating-tag-guard: Infra validation and negative tests reject latest, missing/malformed digests, missing agent source and wrong project identity.
verified_acceptance.rollback-contract: The runbook and local preflight evidence preserve prior image/container metadata, Compose sources and a stopped legacy Beszel-agent rollback anchor without pruning or deleting persistence.
verified_acceptance.runtime-provenance: Main containers report project heim-utilities and canonical /home/alex/repos/infra Compose source; agent reports project heim-utilities-beszel-agent and the installed canonical source.
verified_acceptance.post-rollout-smoke: Digest/provenance verifier, HTTP checks, PostgreSQL pg_isready, Redis PONG, unchanged Paperless counts and growing Beszel rows all passed; browser services remain loopback-bound.

metadata.self_review: PASS: verification is bound to Bureau PR #406, Infra PRs #113/#114, exact heads/merges, implementation diff hash and live runtime evidence. It does not claim future image security, cleanup permission, public-ingress proof or completion of unrelated UTIL-BASE-V1-T013.
